# AWS CloudWatch OTLP Batch Split Processor

## Overview

The AWS CloudWatch OTLP Batch Split Processor splits oversized OTLP log batches into smaller sub-batches that fit within a configurable request size limit. This is useful when exporting to endpoints with request size constraints, such as the [AWS CloudWatch OTLP endpoint](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/CloudWatch-OTLPEndpoint.html) which has a 1MB request limit.

If a single log record exceeds the limit on its own, it is exported as a batch of one.

## Configuration

```yaml
processors:
  awscwotlpbatchsplit:
    max_request_byte_size: 1048576  # default: 1MB
```

| Setting | Description | Default |
| ------- | ----------- | ------- |
| `max_request_byte_size` | Maximum size in bytes for each sub-batch. | `1048576` (1MB) |

### Example

```yaml
receivers:
  otlp:

processors:
  awscwotlpbatchsplit:

exporters:
  otlphttp:
    endpoint: https://xray.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/traces

service:
  pipelines:
    logs:
      receivers: [otlp]
      processors: [awscwotlpbatchsplit]
      exporters: [otlphttp]
```

## How It Works

1. For each incoming log batch, the processor estimates the serialized size of each log record.
2. Log records are grouped into sub-batches that stay within `max_request_byte_size`.
3. Each sub-batch preserves the original resource and scope metadata.
4. Sub-batches are forwarded to the next consumer in the pipeline.
